mechanism_narrative: A sustained three-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces short-term discount rates and stimulates equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: The mean 5-day forward return following three-month declines in FEDFUNDS is not significantly greater than zero over any 3-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 8.5 percent indicates substantial labor market slack that suppresses wage-push inflation expectations and supports corporate margin expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days as monetary policy remains accommodative.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 8.5%, or the mean forward return is not positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month increase in industrial production and decrease in the federal funds rate signals a productivity-enhancing monetary easing that boosts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising INDPRO and falling FEDFUNDS fails to produce positive mean 20-day returns (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction term in a bivariate regression has a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, month-over-month declines in retail sales signal weakening consumer demand that reduces expected corporate revenues and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where CPIAUCSL 3-month change exceeds 1.5%, declining RSAFS is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases, or the mean return is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in housing starts above six hundred thousand units signals durable construction demand that translates into broad economic momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 600 thousand, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observed instances, or the mean return is not positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in payroll employment and industrial production signal synchronized supply and demand expansion that lifts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive month-over-month changes in PAYEMS and INDPRO fail to generate positive mean 5-day S&P 500 returns (t-test p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.25 percent, month-over-month increases in consumer prices signal reflation that reduces real debt burdens and supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: In periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.25%, month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances, or the mean return is not positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP signals aggregate demand expansion that lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly increases in GDPC1 are followed by negative mean 60-day S&P 500 returns (t-test p < 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window, or the positive return frequency falls below 55%.
